01.title: Pair two devices
01.caption: A mints a PAKE code, B claims it; both derive the same channel — no key crosses the server.

02.title: Devices: list / rename / forget
02.caption: And the regression guard: forgetting one device must NOT wipe another device's granted shell cap.
02.detail: rename+forget worked; laptop's shell cap SURVIVED the unrelated forget

03.title: Send with a one-time code
03.caption: Sender mints a speakable code; the receiver claims it and the bytes land, sha256-verified end-to-end.

04.title: Send --to a known device
04.caption: No code: a remembered device, identity proof-verified and auto-accepted; bytes sha256-verified.
04.detail: no code; verified 'laptop'; delivered + hash match

05.title: Always-on receiver: up / status / down
05.caption: Bring up a trusted-only drop target, send into it, check status, stop it; bytes sha256-verified.
05.detail: up received; status reported; down stopped it

06.title: Grant shell + ssh over the tunnel
06.caption: Deny-by-default consent; then `filament ssh peer -- echo OK` runs over the data channel.
06.detail: shell granted; ssh ran a remote command over the tunnel

07.title: Introduce two devices
07.caption: A hub that knows both vouches them to each other with a fresh mutual secret.
07.detail: introduce delivered a fresh mutual secret; alice<->bob now know each other

08.title: CLI sends → the web app receives
08.caption: The browser (local frontend) accepts the offered file and reaches the download affordance.
08.detail: CLI offered invoice.pdf; browser accepted and reached the download (save) affordance

09.title: The web app sends → CLI recv
09.caption: Browser picks a file and sends it; the CLI receiver writes it, sha256-verified (authoritative no-recorder verify pass). The GIF is a best-effort visual; single-host browser→CLI WebRTC can't complete while the webm recorder runs — see README.
09.detail: VERIFY pass (no recorder): sha256 MATCHES. GIF is a best-effort visual; single-host browser→CLI WebRTC can't complete while the webm recorder runs (recording-contention, not a product break).

10.title: Pair the web app with the CLI
10.caption: CLI mints a PAKE code; the browser claims it and stores the device (key never crosses the server).
10.detail: browser claimed the CLI's PAKE code; both sides stored the mutual device
